rationale: This is exceptional profile writing. Every sentence earns its place. The opening image of hands returning to the wheel establishes character through physical detail rather than exposition. Specificity is outstanding — the 1974 wheel, the napkin calculation, the Steinway in 1998, the ratchet straps and moving blankets, the bay 'the color of an old dime.' The language is plain and muscular without a single inflated word. The structure flows naturally from physical observation to biographical context to the captain's own voice, all within a compact space. The piece reads like the best of long-form magazine journalism — it has the texture and authority of writers like John McPhee or Susan Orlean. The only limitation is that it's clearly incomplete (cuts off mid-quote), but what exists on the page is virtually flawless in craft.
